title: indigofera cornuligera
intro: Indigofera cornuligera, also known as kala namak, is a plant species native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. It is a shrub or small tree that has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Indigofera cornuligera include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels. Herbal preparations of Indigofera cornuligera can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and it is often used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
